title: Builder Market Share Exact Breakdown
why_now: This slice produced bounded estimates of builder concentration. Exact per-builder block counts via GROUP BY on builder pubkey would yield precise HHI and top-N share, replacing the current ranges with ground truth.

title: Builder Churn and Temporal Stability
why_now: Single-day snapshot cannot capture builder entry/exit or market share volatility. Extending to a multi-day or multi-week window would reveal whether concentration is stable or regime-dependent.

title: Builder Win Rate from Bid Traces
why_now: This slice showed 109 bidding builders vs 47 delivering builders. Joining bid trace and delivery data for the same time window would reveal per-builder win rates and competitive dynamics.

title: Per-Slot Bid Distribution Analysis
why_now: This slice derived mean bids per slot (~1,181) from aggregates. Per-slot GROUP BY on bid trace would reveal the actual distribution shape — variance, outlier slots, and whether high-MEV slots attract disproportionately more bids.

title: Bid Timing Within Slots
why_now: This slice inferred progressive bid updating from structural evidence. Direct measurement of bid submission timestamps relative to slot boundaries would confirm or refute the last-look hypothesis and quantify the timing distribution.

title: Same-Day Bid-to-Delivery Win Rate
why_now: This slice compared bidders (2024-09-13) to delivered builders (2026-03-29, different date). Joining bid trace and delivery data for the same date would yield exact per-builder win rates, eliminating the date mismatch caveat.

title: Builder-Stratified Public Visibility
why_now: This slice showed ~64.7% overall public visibility but could not stratify by builder due to date mismatch. Temporally aligned mempool + relay delivery data would reveal whether builders with known private order flow relationships show lower public visibility rates.

title: Temporal Variation in Public-Private Ratio
why_now: The ~64.7% public visibility estimate is from a single day. Multi-day or multi-week analysis would reveal whether the ratio is stable or varies with gas prices, MEV conditions, or time of day.

title: Transaction-Type Stratification of Public Visibility
why_now: This slice treated all transactions uniformly. DEX trades, high-value transfers, and contract deployments likely have different public visibility profiles. Stratifying by transaction type would identify which classes are most susceptible to private routing.
